title: GHK-Cu Injectable: Copper Peptide Collagen Signaling
titleHtml: GHK-Cu Injectable: Copper Peptide Collagen Signaling.
deck: The tripeptide glycyl-L-histidyl-L-lysine bound to copper modulates wound healing, collagen synthesis, and gene expression — injectable and topical uses in longevity medicine.
metaDescription: GHK-Cu injectable peptide: copper peptide mechanism, collagen remodeling, 1-2mg daily protocols, topical vs injectable, and research status.
bodyHtml:
GHK-Cu (copper peptide) is a naturally occurring tripeptide that declines with age. It stimulates collagen and glycosaminoglycan synthesis, supports superoxide dismutase activity, and modulates hundreds of genes toward a regenerative expression profile in cell culture studies.

Injectable subcutaneous protocols (1 to 2 mg daily) appear in compounding formularies alongside topical skin products. Human anti-aging RCT data are limited; mechanism is strong in vitro. Copper balance matters — excess can be pro-oxidant.

CLYR Health offers GHK-Cu injectable as a peptides preview SKU, often paired with topical estriol-GHK-Cu creams for women.

sources.label: Pickart L, et al. GHK-Cu and tissue remodeling. Biochim Biophys Acta. 2015.
Pickart L, Margolina A. Regenerative and protective actions of GHK-Cu. Biomed Res Int. 2018.
